questions.question_text: How often must Maryland real estate licensees renew their licenses?
distractors: Every year
Every 3 years
Every 4 years
questions.explanation: Maryland real estate licenses operate on a 2-year renewal cycle. Licensees must complete required continuing education and submit renewal applications before their license expires.

questions.question_text: How many total hours of continuing education must a Maryland salesperson complete per renewal cycle?
distractors: 12 hours
18 hours
24 hours
questions.explanation: Maryland requires 15 hours of continuing education per 2-year renewal cycle. This includes mandatory components for fair housing, legislative updates, and ethics.

questions.question_text: Starting October 2025, how many hours of fair housing education are required in each CE cycle?
distractors: 1 hour
3 hours
4 hours
questions.explanation: Effective October 2025, Maryland requires 2 hours of fair housing education as part of the 15-hour CE requirement. This reflects the state's emphasis on fair housing compliance.

questions.question_text: A salesperson completed only 10 of 15 required CE hours before her renewal deadline. What is the consequence?
distractors: She receives a 90-day grace period
She pays a late fee and continues practicing
Her license converts to inactive automatically
questions.explanation: Failure to complete all required CE hours by the renewal deadline results in license expiration. The licensee cannot practice until the education is completed and the license is properly renewed.

questions.question_text: A broker's CE requirements include a component not required of salespersons. What is this additional topic?
distractors: Advanced appraisal methods of 3 hours
Commercial leasing of 2 hours
Business planning of 1 hour
questions.explanation: Brokers must complete 1.5 hours of supervision training in addition to the standard CE requirements. This reflects their responsibility for overseeing salespersons and associate brokers.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves one brokerage and joins another. Within how many days must the MREC be notified?
distractors: 5 days
15 days
30 days
questions.explanation: Maryland law requires notification to the MREC within 10 days of any change in broker affiliation. This ensures the Commission's records accurately reflect where each licensee is practicing.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to transfer her license to a new broker. What approval is needed?
distractors: Only written consent from both brokers
No approval needed if she notifies within 10 days
Approval from the Department of Labor
questions.explanation: License transfers in Maryland require Commission approval. Simply notifying the MREC is not sufficient; the transfer must be formally approved to ensure regulatory compliance.

questions.question_text: A licensee wants to take a break from real estate but keep her license. She places her license on inactive status. What limitations apply?
questions.correct_answer: She cannot practice and needs no broker affiliation
distractors: She can still close pending transactions
She must maintain a broker affiliation
She must complete CE but cannot list property
questions.explanation: Inactive status means the licensee cannot engage in any licensed real estate activity. However, she does not need to be affiliated with a broker while inactive, providing flexibility for career breaks.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's broker unexpectedly closes the firm. The salesperson continues showing homes while searching for a new broker. Is this permissible?
questions.correct_answer: No, she cannot practice without a broker affiliation
distractors: Yes, for up to 30 days while transitioning
Yes, if she notifies the MREC immediately
Only if she limits activity to existing clients
questions.explanation: A salesperson cannot practice real estate without an active broker affiliation. When the broker relationship ends, all licensed activity must stop until a new affiliation is established and approved.

questions.question_text: An associate broker on inactive status receives an offer to join a new firm. What must happen before she can practice?
questions.correct_answer: Reactivate the license and affiliate with a broker
distractors: Complete 60 hours of pre-license education again
Pass the state exam a second time
Obtain written approval from her former broker
questions.explanation: A licensee on inactive status cannot practice real estate. She must reactivate her license by meeting any outstanding CE requirements and then establish a new broker affiliation before resuming practice.

questions.question_text: A real estate brokerage firm in Maryland must designate which type of licensee to oversee operations?
distractors: Managing salesperson
Compliance officer
Office administrator
questions.explanation: Every real estate business entity in Maryland must have a qualifying broker who is responsible for overseeing operations and ensuring compliance with real estate laws and regulations.

questions.question_text: Three investors want to form a real estate brokerage LLC in Maryland. Only one holds a real estate license. Does this meet the ownership requirement?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, one-third of owners hold RE licenses
distractors: No, all owners must be licensed
No, at least two-thirds must be licensed
Yes, only the qualifying broker needs a license
questions.explanation: Maryland requires at least one-third of a real estate business entity's owners to hold real estate licenses. With one of three owners licensed, this LLC meets the minimum threshold.

questions.question_text: A brokerage operates from a home office that is not accessible to the public. Does this comply with Maryland law?
questions.correct_answer: No, the office must be accessible to the public
distractors: Yes, home offices are permitted without restriction
Yes, if the broker registers the address with MREC
Only if no client meetings occur at that location
questions.explanation: Maryland requires brokerages to maintain a definite place of business that is accessible to the public. A home office that cannot be accessed by the public does not meet this requirement.

questions.question_text: A broker opens a second office location in another county. What must she do regarding this branch?
questions.correct_answer: Register the branch office separately with the MREC
distractors: Nothing, if a licensed agent staffs the location
Obtain a separate broker license for that county
File a zoning application with the county
questions.explanation: Maryland requires each branch office to be separately registered with the MREC. This ensures the Commission knows about and can oversee all locations where licensed activity occurs.

questions.question_text: A real estate corporation applies for a license from the MREC. One of its six shareholders holds a real estate license. Does this meet the ownership requirement?
questions.correct_answer: No, at least one-third of owners must be licensed
distractors: Yes, only one licensed owner is needed
Yes, only the qualifying broker must be licensed
No, all shareholders must hold licenses
questions.explanation: Maryland requires at least one-third of a business entity's owners to hold real estate licenses. One of six shareholders is approximately 17%, which falls below the one-third (33%) threshold.
